codeReview.summary: The feature adds a clean shared contract module and comprehensive tests, but envelope validation does not fully enforce the declared contract shape for nullable fields.

issues.description: `isValidOptionalId` accepts `undefined`, and `validateEnvelope` relies on it for `in_reply_to`, `dedupe_key`, and `trace_id` (src/contract/envelope.ts:96,101,106). This allows missing fields to pass even though the contract/messages require these fields to be present as `null` or non-empty string, so invalid envelope shapes are not deterministically rejected.

ID validation only checks non-empty strings, so `id`/`thread_id` validators do not enforce the documented typed prefixes (`msg_`, `thr_`, etc.) even though the module comments describe those as contract semantics.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mors-worker skill still demonstrates Jest-style `--testPathPattern` usage, but this repo uses Vitest and that flag is invalid.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/mors-worker/SKILL.md:42 shows `npm run test -- --testPathPattern init`; transcript shows worker tried `npm run test -- --testPathPattern contract` and then switched to `npx vitest run test/contract.test.ts`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature verification instructions also use Jest-style test filtering, which caused avoidable command churn during implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature `core-envelope-and-state-contracts` verificationSteps include `npm run test -- --testPathPattern contract`; transcript skeleton records that command failing before the worker used a Vitest-compatible command.

summary: Reviewed commit ca118cf and transcript/handoff evidence. Contract module structure and transition logic are strong, but envelope validation currently permits undefined nullable fields, so the feature does not fully satisfy strict contract-shape enforcement.
